div.p: H_new = compress(H_old, block) + H_old The + (mod 2³²) is not cosmetic — it is the load-bearing one-way ratchet that makes the compression function irreversible:

ul.li: Without feed-forward: compress is a block cipher E(key=H_old, pt=block). Given ciphertext (H_new) and key (H_old), you can invert it.

With feed-forward: inversion requires compress⁻¹(H_new − H_old), which entangles the block cipher inverse with the unknown H_old. No known efficient algorithm exists. Single character. Maximum impact. = vs +=

div.p: Working vars a..h hold the compression output of block i — derived from H_old + 64 rounds over W[0..63]
☠️ ATTACKER WINS Prior block state 0x6a09e667… is discarded entirely Controlling Block N → controls the entire digest Length-extension trivially possible: resume from any known H state

🛡️ ATTACKER LOSES Davies-Meyer feed-forward: H_new = f(H_old, block) + H_old Every prior block's entropy is irreversibly folded into H via modular addition Changing ANY prior block changes H_old → cascades through ALL subsequent blocks Cannot control digest without controlling the full message chain

g.text: Final H[0..7] — big-endian serialized → 64 hex chars
H[0..7] holds ONLY last block's output
